com.mentor.chs.plugin.task

Interface IXTask

  • All Superinterfaces:
    IXPlugin

    public interface IXTask
extends IXPlugin
    This is the plugin interface for custom tasks.

    A custom task is a task for which implementation is provided by users and get executed in background (for example it can be executed at Capital Integration Server instance in a remote machine) All the custom tasks must implement this interface.

    • Nested Class Summary

      Nested Classes 
      Modifier and Type Interface and Description
      static class  IXTask.AbortStatus 

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Abstract Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void abort(IXTask.AbortStatus status)
      This method is called when task is aborted so that plugin have the opportunity to cleanly release an expensive resource connection or file before the task is aborted.
      boolean run(IXTaskProgress progress, IXTaskHTMLLogger logger, java.io.InputStream inputStream, IXTaskContext taskContext, IXSystemContext systemContext)
      This is the API invoked on custom tasks providing all necessary objects passed as parameters.

      • Methods inherited from interface com.mentor.chs.plugin.IXPlugin

        getDescription, getName, getVersion

    • Method Detail

      • run

        boolean run(IXTaskProgress progress,
            IXTaskHTMLLogger logger,
            java.io.InputStream inputStream,
            IXTaskContext taskContext,
            IXSystemContext systemContext)
        This is the API invoked on custom tasks providing all necessary objects passed as parameters.
        Parameters:
        progress - - progress object that custom task should update. Progress object can be updated to indicate the framework of actual progress of the custom task
        logger - - output that custom task can write to.
        inputStream - - parameters stream providing the control parameters of the task
        taskContext - - context specific to the task (for example task id, ability to access/modify task attachments)
        systemContext - - context providing access to the data objects
        Returns:
        boolean - result of execution of the custom task - true indicates success and failure otherwise

      • abort

        void abort(IXTask.AbortStatus status)
        This method is called when task is aborted so that plugin have the opportunity to cleanly release an expensive resource connection or file before the task is aborted. Possible abort cases are cancelling a running task or task failed due to some internal error\exception. Cancelling a running task is not yet supported but might be implemented in future..
        Parameters:
        status - - AbortStatus specifying whether task is aborted due to cancellation of the task or due to some errors
